paragraphs: Imagine a contract: for every 'one' spotted, the subject gets $100. A hundred people participate. Ninety-nine of them spot about a hundred 'ones' each and walk away with $10,000. Pure joy and universal celebration. But one person ignores the 'ones' entirely and focuses on the 'zeros'. He is paid $1,000,000 without any explanation. The ninety-nine are in shock and absolute rage! They are ready to tear both the experimenter and the lucky guy apart! And yet... the contract was never broken, was it? The outrage over this 'injustice' is entirely groundless.

Why am I bringing this up? Because I despise living by the manual. I reject consumer society and the state. But I don't want the million, either. I just want to keep looking at the Universe through these eyes and exploring. To listen and actually hear! To look and truly see! To marvel, to admire, to face disappointment, to laugh, and to cry. And most importantly, I believe the Universe wants exactly the same.
